page: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads. It is equipped with 42.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage is included, though the instance is premium storage capable, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The high clock speed of 4.2 GHz drives top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2335.0 and strong single-threaded compression and decompression results. However, because the instance is limited to a single physical core, multi-core performance is weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score in the bottom 10% and a Geekbench multi-core score in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth also presents a bottleneck, with uncached read and write speeds falling into the bottom 10% tier, although cached small-block reads perform slightly better.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the FX2ms_v2 offers a specialized resource profile that avoids the expense of scaling up vCPU counts simply to acquire more RAM. This makes it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require heavy parallel processing. Suitable workloads include single-threaded text processing, lightweight database operations, and applications requiring high memory capacity alongside strong single-core execution, while it is poorly suited for highly parallelized multi-threaded tasks or high-throughput local storage operations.
description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on a single physical Intel Xeon 8573C core running at 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 42.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per vCPU, but lacks local storage. Benchmarks highlight a significant performance tradeoff: the instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, with strong single-threaded text processing and compression, but exhibits poor multi-core performance and weak uncached memory bandwidth due to its limited core count. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads that do not scale across multiple threads. It is best suited for single-threaded memory-intensive applications, light database caching, and text processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 42.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor at 4.2 GHz, offering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs, 42 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine pairing high-frequency single-core performance with an exceptional memory-to-vCPU ratio for specialized workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
42.0 GB of RAM providing 21.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling
No bundled local storage with premium storage capability
Best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads
